ci._comment: Lighthouse CI config. Audits the most important public surfaces — anything a first-time visitor or a search crawler is most likely to hit. Numbers are hardware-aware (LH runs in headless Chrome on a GitHub-hosted Linux VM, scoring tends to come in 5-10 points lower than a Lighthouse run on the same site from a fast laptop). Thresholds reflect that.

settings._skipAudits_comment: Drop the audits that are noisy on third-party-heavy pages (uses-rel-preconnect for fonts.gstatic.com is unavoidable; bootup-time on a cold cache fluctuates).

assertions._comment_per_metric: Per-metric thresholds for the metrics that matter to conversion.
assertions._comment_a11y_floor: A11y is held to a higher bar than perf. Anything below 90 means a gradient card has bad contrast, a focus ring is missing, or a form field has no label.
label: error
